body.title: [sprint] ALARMS PANEL — conditional, all web pages + status panels (the loud visual channel)
body.intention: I intend that an open system alarm is impossible to miss in the web UI — a panel that only appears when there IS an alarm, on every page, plus the per-device status panels.
body.description:
**Problem:** email/push is dead (google_secretary off). The loud channel for a new alarm must be VISUAL. An open alarm should be impossible to miss across the web UI.

**Shape:** An **ALARMS PANEL** that renders ONLY when >=1 open alarm exists, and appears on ALL web pages. It lists open alarms from the flat-file store (signature, aggregate count, caller breakdown = the punch-list, last_seen). Surface alarm presence on the per-device **status panels** (Akien, CC, Skeleton, Vetinari) and the **Control Station** panel. Read the store DIRECTLY (unseen_university.system_alarms.list_alarms) — no dependency on the alarm-raiser or DB being up, preserving the primitive's resilience. Add a GET /api/alarms endpoint; the panel polls it. Optional: a close control that calls close_alarm(signature).

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (monolithic web app — panel component, /api/alarms, all-pages injection, status-panel hooks). Reads unseen_university/system_alarms.py.
**Design rules:** zero new backend deps; read flat-file store directly (resilient); conditional render (no panel when no alarms); log the crossing (/api/alarms reads).
**Scope boundary:** IN — /api/alarms + the conditional ALARMS PANEL on all pages + alarm indicator on the named status panels. OUT — tmux nag (T-system-alarms-tmux-nag); the primitive + fatal (shipped); deciding WHICH events raise alarms.
**Test plan:** with no open alarms, /api/alarms returns empty and the panel does not render; after raise_alarm drops one, /api/alarms returns it with caller breakdown and the panel renders on an arbitrary page; closing it removes it from /api/alarms. (server.py is large — a focused endpoint test + a render-guard test; full UI is manual.)
**Completion criteria:** GET /api/alarms reflects list_alarms() (open only, with caller breakdown); the ALARMS PANEL renders iff alarms exist and is injected on all pages; at least the Control Station + one device status panel show an alarm indicator. NOTE: server.py is HIGH-traffic/monolithic — needs a web-UI orientation pass + Akien sign-off on the panel UX before build.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
